questions.text: Plants that thrive on highly saline soils and are deliberately used for soil stabilisation in coastal defence zones are known as
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Halophytes (C)**

Halophytes (e.g., mangroves, Suaeda, Salicornia) possess specialised adaptations like salt-excreting glands, succulence, and vivipary to tolerate high NaCl levels (up to 40 ppt). In DRDO CEPTAM-11 syllabus, this concept is critical under plant adaptations and economic botany as India’s 7,500 km coastline requires halophyte-based bio-shields for erosion control, cyclone protection, and naval base camouflage. DRDO’s coastal greening projects use halophytes for bioremediation of saline soils near naval installations.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **Xerophytes (A):** Adapted to arid deserts (deep roots, reduced leaves) — irrelevant to saline waterlogging.
• **Hydrophytes (B):** Fully aquatic plants (e.g., lotus) — cannot tolerate high salt.
• **Epiphytes (D):** Grow on other plants for support only (e.g., orchids) — no salinity tolerance link.

**Important Exam Concept:** Defence applications of halophytes in coastal warfare & environmental engineering (frequently asked in DRDO General Science).

questions.text: Plants that grow on other plants solely for mechanical support without deriving nutrients or water from the host are classified as
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Epiphytes (D)**

Epiphytes (e.g., orchids, mosses) use aerial roots or velamen for anchorage and absorb atmospheric moisture. DRDO relevance: biomimicry in lightweight camouflage netting and UAV design (aerial root structures for sensor mounting) and forest concealment studies in Northeast border areas.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **Halophytes (A):** Saline-soil specialists.
• **Xerophytes (B):** Drought-adapted.
• **Hydrophytes (C):** Water-dwelling.

**Important Exam Concept:** Convergent evolution & biomimicry for defence technology (high-weightage in taxonomy & applied biology).

questions.text: The maximum amount of water required by plants for growth is absorbed through
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Root hairs (A)**

Root hairs (extensions of epidermal cells) increase surface area 10–100 fold for passive water absorption via osmosis. DRDO context: critical for soldier hydration strategies in desert operations and hydroponics for forward-area rations.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **Lenticels (B):** Gaseous exchange in stems.
• **Stomata (C):** Transpiration & CO₂ intake.
• **Cuticle (D):** Prevents water loss.

**Important Exam Concept:** Plant physiology & water relations in extreme environments (repeated in DRDO papers).

questions.text: Plants that are adapted to live completely submerged or floating in water are termed
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Hydrophytes (D)**

Hydrophytes (e.g., Hydrilla, Eichhornia) show aerenchyma, reduced cuticle, and air sacs. DRDO applications: water purification in naval bases and aquatic camouflage studies.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **Halophytes (A):** Saline soil.
• **Epiphytes (B):** Support only.
• **Xerophytes (C):** Dry habitats.

**Important Exam Concept:** Aquatic adaptations & ecosystem management (naval & border ecology).

questions.text: The radiant energy absorbed by chlorophyll during photosynthesis primarily results in
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Photolysis of water (C)**

Light energy splits water (2H₂O → 4H⁺ + 4e⁻ + O₂) in PS-II, releasing oxygen. DRDO relevance: understanding oxygen production for submarine life-support systems and bio-regenerative air revitalisation.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **Oxidation of carbon dioxide (A):** Reverse of photosynthesis.
• **Reduction of oxygen (B):** Respiration.
• **Formation of starch (D):** Dark reaction end-product.

**Important Exam Concept:** Light reaction & oxygen evolution (core photosynthesis for DRDO environmental science).

questions.text: In an ecological pyramid, the first trophic level consists of
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Green plants (producers) (D)**

Autotrophs fix solar energy. DRDO context: understanding energy flow for food security in high-altitude border posts and bioenergy from algae.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **Carnivores/Herbivores/Omnivores (A,B,C):** Higher trophic levels.

**Important Exam Concept:** Trophic levels & energy pyramids (ecology for defence sustainability).

questions.text: A group of archaebacteria capable of producing methane gas in anaerobic conditions is used in
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Biogas production (B)**

Methanogens (Methanobacterium) convert organic waste to CH₄. DRDO uses biogas plants for clean energy in remote military camps.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others unrelated to archaebacteria.

**Important Exam Concept:** Extremophiles & renewable energy for field operations.

questions.text: Most insects respire through a network of air tubes called
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Tracheal system (C)**

Direct diffusion via spiracles and tracheoles. DRDO biomimicry: micro-air channels in lightweight soldier gear and UAV cooling systems.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Gills (A): Aquatic. Lungs (B): Vertebrates. Skin (D): Earthworms.

**Important Exam Concept:** Respiratory adaptations & bio-inspired engineering.

questions.text: Fishes survive under a frozen lake because
options: Water at the bottom does not freeze
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Water at the bottom does not freeze (C)**

Ice floats; bottom water remains at 4°C. DRDO naval research uses this for cold-water diving physiology.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others factually incorrect.

**Important Exam Concept:** Anomalous water expansion & aquatic survival (naval ops).

questions.text: Fish generally respire through
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Gills (C)**

Counter-current exchange of O₂. DRDO: gill-mimicry in underwater breathing apparatus and sonar studies.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others not respiratory organs.

**Important Exam Concept:** Aquatic respiration & naval technology.

questions.text: The red colour in ripe apples is primarily due to
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Anthocyanin (B)**

pH-dependent flavonoid pigment. DRDO: natural food colourants for troop rations.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Lycopene (A): Tomato. Carotene (C): Carrot. Xanthophyll (D): Yellow leaves.

**Important Exam Concept:** Plant pigments & nutritional security.

questions.text: Red colour in tomato is due to the pigment
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Lycopene (B)**

Carotenoid antioxidant. DRDO: antioxidant-rich supplements for soldiers.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others incorrect pigments.

**Important Exam Concept:** Carotenoids & health in defence rations.

questions.text: Yellow colour of papaya is due to
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Caricaxanthin (C)**

Specific xanthophyll. DRDO: natural colourants for packaged foods.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others from different fruits.

**Important Exam Concept:** Fruit pigments & economic botany.

questions.text: Opium is commercially obtained from which part of the poppy plant?
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Unripe capsules (C)**

Latex from incisions on green capsules yields morphine. DRDO medical research: controlled alkaloids for pain management in field hospitals.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others yield no opium.

**Important Exam Concept:** Economic botany & pharmacognosy (defence medicine).

questions.text: All human beings (Chinese, Indians, Africans, Europeans) belong to the same
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Species (C)**

Homo sapiens. DRDO human factors research: uniform physiological standards for diverse troops.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Broader taxa include multiple species.

**Important Exam Concept:** Biological species concept & human evolution.

questions.text: Yeast is an example of
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Fungus (C)**

Unicellular ascomycete. DRDO: fermentation for biofuel & food preservation in rations.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others incorrect kingdoms.

**Important Exam Concept:** Fungi classification & industrial microbiology.

questions.text: Yeast and mushrooms both belong to the group
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Fungi (B)**

Heterotrophic, chitinous cell wall. DRDO: mushroom cultivation for protein-rich soldier diet and mycorrhizal biofertilizers.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Algae photosynthetic; others unrelated.

**Important Exam Concept:** Kingdom Fungi & applied mycology.

questions.text: Mushroom used as a vegetable is actually a
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Fungus (B)**

Basidiomycete fruiting body. DRDO: protein source for high-altitude rations.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others not edible mushrooms.

**Important Exam Concept:** Edible fungi & nutritional security.

questions.text: Major component of cotton fibre is
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Cellulose (B)**

β-1,4 glucose polymer. DRDO textile division uses this for uniforms, parachutes, and bullet-proof vests.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Protein (A): silk. Starch (C): food. Lignin (D): wood.

**Important Exam Concept:** Plant fibres & defence textiles.

questions.text: Lichens represent a symbiotic association between
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Algae and fungi (B)**

Alga provides food; fungus shelter. DRDO: pioneer species for soil formation in high-altitude border areas and bio-indicators of pollution.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others incorrect partners.

**Important Exam Concept:** Symbiosis & ecological succession (defence environmental science).
